key_insight: BASE formalizes only one candidate answer with Lean and then reuses an edit-based rewrite to generate the remaining candidate statements, reducing cost without sacrificing answer-selection quality.
engineering_relevance: This makes Lean-based test-time answer selection more scalable for math reasoning systems by cutting repeated autoformalization work while preserving verification-based selection.
method: Choose one base candidate per problem and autoformalize it once in Lean.
Train LEANSCRIBE to locate the answer expression and produce a reusable edit function.
Apply the edit in place to derive the other K-1 candidate formalizations instead of re-formalizing each one.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a Pareto improvement across all 12 dataset-solver settings on four benchmarks and three solvers, with about a 5x reduction in autoformalizer calls at K=8.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliably localizing and editing the answer expression in the base formalization, so brittle rewriting or complex statement structure could limit robustness.
paper_title: Formalize Once, Edit the Rest: Efficient Lean-Based Answer Selection for Math Reasoning
